titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 609

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=25 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.240744+00 | translation moa=26 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.227422+00

div.p:
Willelmus Rex Scottorum . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terrę sue clericis ⁊ laicis  salutem. Sciatis quod controuersia que fuit inter ęcclesiam dunel
mensem ⁊ ęcclesiam croilandensem de uilla de hederham ⁊ ecclesia eiusdem uille ⁊ omnibus pertinentiis suis . in presentia mea ⁊ episcoporum ⁊ proborum
hominum meorum terminata est  ita quod uilla ⁊ ecclesia de hederham cum omnibus capellis ⁊ pertinentiis suis ęcclesię dunelmensi cesserit inperpe
tuum. Testibus . Ricardo Episcopo Sancti Andree . Engelr’ episcopo de glasgu . Nichol’ cancellario . Comite Wald’ . Ricardo de moræuilla constabulario .
Waltero filio alani Dapifero . DD’. olifard . Apud pert .

William king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Know that the dispute which was between the church of Durham and the church of Crowland about the toun of Edrom and the church of the same toun and all its pertinents has been concluded in my court in the presence of me and of my bishops and worthy men, so that the toun and church of Edrom with all its chapels and pertinents passes to the church of Durham in perpetuity. As witnesses: Richard the bishop of St Andrews, Ingram the bishop of Glasgow, Nicholas the chancellor, John the abbot of Kelso, the Earl Waldef, Richard de Morville the constable, Walter son of Alan, Dapifer [Steward], David Olifard [Oliphant]. At Perth.
